core_philosophy.rule: Prefer dense, scannable tools over decorative layouts.
Do not repeat the same title across chrome, panel, workspace, and card.
avoid: breadcrumb plus title plus card title for the same object
section headers where row labels already explain the content

core_philosophy.rule: Do not show disabled or placeholder controls unless they explain a real unavailable state.
avoid: hidden future buttons in visible UI
disabled New/Add/Help buttons with no implementation
reserved tabs that do not open useful surfaces

core_philosophy.rule: The blank profile must contain no project content, review route state, sample data, or default taxonomy.
avoid: fallback project names inside runtime state

core_philosophy.rule: Design for long low-light work sessions and sensitive eyes.
avoid: theme colors hard-coded outside style tokens

core_philosophy.rule: Panels exist to hold useful tools, navigation, inspection, or logs.
avoid: panel chrome that consumes more space than panel content

core_philosophy.rule: Every visible object should support navigation, inspection, action, review, or feedback.
Reusable improvements belong in the shared shell; project-specific demands belong in profiles, data, or feature modules.
avoid: hard-coding one project's vocabulary in shell files
one-off layout hacks that bypass surface contracts

dex_roles.purpose: Define boundaries, contracts, and integration plans.
Implement scoped UI and data changes.
must_not_do: read the whole repo unless blocked

dex_roles.purpose: Find regressions, slop, and contract violations.
Extract project knowledge and classify it for UI integration.
must_not_do: dump raw repo context into the shell
recommend UI content without a surface target
blur project data with shared shell behavior

dex_roles.purpose: Maintain color, typography, and low-light comfort.

design_profiles.purpose: Reusable empty shell for a new project.
Route, artifact, or decision review with notes and inspector data.
Project knowledge, agent notes, code snippets, and docs.
Image, palette, layer, and generated-asset review.
Read-only or scratch text/code review workspace.
Configure theme, panels, features, and project behavior.

review_checks.question: Does more than one visible title name the same selected object?
review_checks.fail_if: The left panel, top chrome, workspace, and focus panel repeat the same title.

review_checks.question: Are there visible controls that do nothing or exist only for future work?
review_checks.fail_if: A disabled placeholder control is visible without a real current state.

review_checks.question: Are there boxes inside boxes where background color alone would separate content?
review_checks.fail_if: A surface uses panels/cards as decoration instead of grouping active content.

review_checks.question: Does the blank profile show or load project/review content?
review_checks.fail_if: Blank mode has route data, sample rows, review text, or placeholder documents.

review_checks.question: Did a feature hard-code visual colors instead of using theme tokens?
review_checks.fail_if: Feature QML introduces project colors without a theme contract.
